channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Timberwolves watch their playoff position tumble after blowing huge lead in 4th quarter
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Regular-season defeats don’t get much more brutal than the one the Minnesota Timberwolves suffered Tuesday. The wide-open nature of the Western Conference playoff race doesn’t give them much time to stew over what went wrong.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: With top seed in East secured, Atkinson wants to get some of Cavaliers' top players some rest
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Kenny Atkinson thought during training camp that realistic goals for the Cleveland Cavaliers would be 50 wins and a top-three seed in the Eastern Conference. The Cavaliers surpassed those goals awhile ago to the point where Atkinson is planning for a possible championship run.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Swanson hits tiebreaking single in 4-run eighth as the Cubs beat the Rangers 10-6
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Dansby Swanson lined a tiebreaking two-run single in Chicago's four-run eighth inning, and the Cubs beat the Texas Rangers 10-6 on Tuesday on another frigid night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Grizzlies’ Jaylen Wells stretchered off court after falling on head due to hard foul by Hornets player
item.description: Grizzlies guard Jaylen Wells was stretchered off the court against the Hornets after K.J. Simpson swept his legs during a dunk attempt. Simpson was later ejected for a Flagrant 2 foul.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cavaliers claim the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Bulls
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Darius Garland scored 28 points, Evan Mobley had 21 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists and the Cleveland Cavaliers clinched the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Chicago Bulls on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Octavio Dotel, who once held record of pitching for 13 major league teams, dies in DR roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Octavio Dotel, who pitched for 13 major league teams in a 15-year career and won a World Series with the St. Louis Cardinals, was among the dead after a roof collapsed at a nightclub in his native Dominican Republic where he was attending a merengue concert.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Loyola University Chicago sent preservation notice tied to federally indicted ex-Michigan coach
item.description: Loyola University Chicago has received a preservation notice from attorneys representing a former student-athlete, compelling the school to preserve evidence related to the indictment of former Michigan football assistant coach Matt Weiss on unauthorized computer access and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Former Oakland A’s pitcher Octavio Dotel killed in nightclub roof collapse in Dominican Republic
item.description: At least 58 people died and at least 160 were injured when the roof collapsed at the Jet Set nightclub in Dotel's hometown of Santo Domingo, where a concert was taking place.
item.dc:creator: Mercury News staff and wire reports, The Mercury News

item.title: DI leading scorer Ta'Niya Latson commits to South Carolina
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Ta'Niya Latson, the leading scorer in Division I, has decided to transfer to South Carolina, where she will join a high school teammate and try to help the Gamecocks win another national championship.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, Fox 5

item.title: USA Fencing faces Texas probe after female fencer protests against competing with trans athlete
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton announced an investigation into USA Fencing after fencer Stephanie Turner protested against competing with a transgender athlete.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tiger Woods teams up with Augusta National to build par-3 course at the Patch
item.description: Tiger Woods and Augusta National announced they have teamed up to build a par-3 course at the facility known as the Patch in Augusta, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two courts: NCAA's present (Gators!) and future play out 1700 miles apart on the same day
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The final buzzer in San Antonio closed a drama that ended with confetti and Gator chomps — a thrill-a-minute NCAA title for the Florida Gators that reminded us all of what's so good about the games these college athletes play.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah HC stays alive with 4-1 victory over Winnipeg
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is probably not going to make the NHL playoffs, but they are not going down without a fight. Karel Vejmelka made 32 saves for Utah in a 4-1 win against the the top team in the NHL, the Winnipeg Jets, at Delta Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Carson Kelly homers as the Cubs beat the Padres 7-1 for their 5th straight win
item.description: Carson Kelly homered and drove in four runs, and the Chicago Cubs beat the San Diego Padres 7-1 on Saturday for their fifth consecutive win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carmelo Anthony and Sue Bird highlight Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Fox 5

item.title: Strong, Fudd help UConn blow out UCLA, 85-51, in Final Four as Paige Bueckers moves 1 win from elusive title
item.description: Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd didn’t even need much help from superstar Paige Bueckers to propel UConn into the national title game, leading the Huskies to a remarkably easy 85-51 victory over UCLA on Friday night in the Final Four of the women’s NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, WGN 9

item.title: Keanu Tanuvasa, Carsen Ryan go from Utah to BYU
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Last year at this time, Keanu Tanuvasa and Carsen Ryan were both gearing up for Utah's first season in the Big 12 Conference. Now, they're getting ready for Utah's biggest rival.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Fencer refuses to compete against transgender opponent
item.description: Turner told NewsNation she didn’t “want to let people know that I'm OK with this and pretend like nothing's happening.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Report: Chris Collins agrees to multi-year extension with Northwestern
item.description: Northwestern University men's basketball coach Chris Collins has agreed to a multi-year contract extension, making him one of the top coaches in the Big Ten in terms of salary and compensation.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
